response:
The total response time function combines the individual database costs into one line.  
Each database receives a specific share of the total query load line.  
MySQL processes alpha times the total number of incoming queries line.  
Oracle handles beta times the total number of incoming queries line.  
Sybase receives the remaining portion of the total query load line.  
The sum of these three individual times forms the total function line.  
Optimization requires finding the values for alpha and beta that minimize time line.  
Calculus derivatives help locate the minimum point for the total function line.  
Substituting the given constants into the equations yields specific numerical values line.  
Solving the resulting system of equations provides the optimal distribution ratios line.  
The calculated alpha value is approximately zero point zero four line.  
The calculated beta value is approximately zero point three two line.  
These values ensure the fastest possible average response time for the system line.  
The original model structure remains unchanged during this calculation process line.  
Final verification confirms that no other distribution yields a lower time line.
